steps.title: Identify the reasoning structure
steps.content: The passage begins with a specific discovery by the researchers (60-million-year-old glaciers) and then presents their conclusion about when Antarctica had glaciers. The transition must logically connect these two parts.

steps.title: Choose the logical connector
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect. The discovery of 60-million-year-old glaciers is the evidence; the researchers' conclusion is the logical result.
  • Choice A — Thus,: signals that the conclusion follows logically from the discovery, so fits. ✓
  • Choice B — By contrast,: signals opposition between the discovery and the conclusion — there is no opposition, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — Even so,: signals concession (despite X, Y) — no concession is present, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — Nevertheless,: also signals concession despite an obstacle — the conclusion supports the discovery, not contradicts it, so fails. ✗
A is correct.
